title: Quantum Navigation
intent: Navigation system using quantum entanglement for positioning, merging particle physics with wayfinding to achieve GPS-independent, interference-resistant spatial awareness.

noise_to_insight.△≈: Extreme cooling requirements reframed as environmental selectivity — natural deployment filtering
noise_to_insight.∞: Topological fragility reframed as design frontier — topological qubits provide inherent error immunity
noise_to_insight.◧: Heisenberg limits reframed as information boundary — uncertainty encodes precision ceiling

insight: A compass built from entangled particles, where uncertainty is not the enemy but the instrument — Heisenberg's limit becomes the calibration mark, topological fragility becomes the roadmap to robustness, and the extreme energy cost of coherence becomes a natural filter for meaningful deployment. Navigation without satellites, without signals, without ground — only the quantum fabric of space itself.
